blocks.content: Crystal checked the list for me. She was nowhere near that hall. Jonathan was right about that.
She said congrats like she meant it and then went back to being a senior and I went back to being a freshman and that's how it goes. For now.
The last route. The one I stopped thinking on. That's the one that matters. I ran two hundred routes today and only one of them felt like that.
I need to find that again tomorrow. And the day after.
Larry Jr. asked 'how do you feel' before I knew. He always does that. He asked it about school too on the first day. He doesn't ask what happened — he asks how it felt. That's a different question.
I don't know what's going to happen. I just know today I ran a route right and my name was on a list and none of that existed two weeks ago.
Down the hall Larry Jr.'s light was off. Mr. Cole's too.
The house was enormous and quiet and mine.
First game is in three weeks.
